deployment.name: Public geospatial Q&A · freeform (example)
deployment.description: Public geospatial Q&A with tools defaulting to FREEFORM (raw STAC/stats payloads passed into the LLM context). Same agent + 7-tool pipeline as `public-geospatial-qa.json`, but per-tool RETURN SHAPE = freeform by default — the headline per-query cost is ~8.8× the templated default (at the bundled 10K MAU; the monthly bill lever compresses toward 3–6× at smaller MAU as fixed infra dilutes the per-query savings, per paper §6). Use this preset to model the cost of letting the LLM see raw STAC item arrays + per-band stats arrays directly. Flip individual tools back to templated to see the per-tool lever.

anchor_query.example: Visualize gross primary production (GPP) from 'lis-global-da-gpp' over Mendocino County, California, June to November 2020.

_calibration.source: Measured directly from the public geospatial Q&A reference agent's templated response handlers and a public STAC catalog API on 2026-05-31.
_calibration.method: cap_tokens per tool = tiktoken o200k_base of the agent's templated response strings (e.g., 'Found 92 catalog items (showing 15 of 92...)' = 27 tok for stac_search). result_tokens_avg per tool = tiktoken of the raw public STAC catalog API payload for the same lookup, lis-global-da-gpp / Mendocino County / 2020-06 to 2020-11. stac_search anchored on 15 STAC items × 1,230 tok = 18,435 tok directly measured.

per_turn_input.templated: sysprompt 2814 + agent.input 0 + tool sum 119 = 2933 tok/turn (matches default load)
per_turn_input.freeform: sysprompt 2814 + agent.input 0 + tool sum 20926 = 23740 tok/turn (load preset, flip all tools to freeform via global RETURN SHAPE)

_calibration.supersedes: Earlier paper-stated anchors of 3342 (templated) and 22798 (freeform) were estimates; these measured values are paper-faithful by construction (paper §6 reflects these measured values).

set_datetime.label: parse_datetime
set_datetime.description: Validate and normalize a date range to an ISO-8601 interval. Templated response is the parsed interval + confirmation request.

get_place.label: geocode
get_place.description: Resolve a place name to bbox + admin geometry. Templated returns a short confirmation; freeform returns the full GeoJSON polygon.

collections_rag.label: search_collections
collections_rag.description: Semantic search over the STAC catalog. Templated returns just the count + selection prompt; freeform returns the Top-K collection metadata (descriptions, summaries, asset dictionaries) measured at ~300 tok/collection × 5 collections via the public STAC catalog's /collections endpoint.

select_collection.label: select_collection
select_collection.description: Records the user's collection + variable selection (small either way).

stac_search.label: search_items
stac_search.description: Enumerate catalog items inside the AOI and datetime window. Templated returns just the count; freeform returns the full STAC item array — measured at 15 items × 1,230 tok = 18,435 tok directly from the public STAC catalog's /search endpoint against lis-global-da-gpp over Mendocino County. This is the dominant freeform lever.

stats.label: compute_stats
stats.description: Per-item zonal statistics (mean/median/min/max/percentiles). Templated returns just the count; freeform returns the per-item stats dictionaries (~40 tok/item × 15 items = 600 tok estimated).

viz.label: render_map
viz.description: Build a raster tile URL for the selected items + AOI. Small either way — visualization metadata is bounded.

agents.label: Geo Q&A Agent (server-side ReAct)
agents.description: Single server-side ReAct agent. Per-call input = sysprompt (2,814 cached) + 0 fresh; tool returns contribute via the registry. At freeform default, total per-turn input = 23,740 tok (paper-measured freeform anchor).

mixed.label: Mixed (40% full · 30% rag · 15% partial · 10% refusal · 5% heavy)

segments.label: Public visitors
segments.description: Anonymous public visitors. Single-segment simplified — load public-geospatial-qa-multi-segment.json for the paper's 500-auth + 10K-public split (auth contributes ~1.6% of monthly queries).

phases.label: Year 1 — pilot on API
phases.description: Low-risk start. Validate workload + cache hit rate against real usage before committing to spend.

phases.label: Year 2 — committed-spend reservation
phases.description: Lock in a discount with Azure PTU or OpenAI Enterprise commit once volume is steady.

phases.label: Year 3 — hybrid 50/50
phases.description: Stand up Llama-based self-host for steady baseline traffic; keep API for spikes and tail queries.
